# Config

> A modern, header-only C++20 configuration management library backed by nlohmann/json with a zero-boilerplate global API and a full-featured ConfigStore class.

The library is entirely header-only — drop `include/config` onto your include path and `#include <config/config.hpp>`. The central type is `ConfigStore`, which wraps a single JSON file and exposes typed get/set, persistence strategies, layered loading, change listeners, and a background file watcher. A parallel set of global free functions (in the `config::` namespace) delegates to a lazily-created default store so simple programs need zero setup. All reads and writes are guarded by `std::shared_mutex` for lock-free concurrent reads and exclusive writes. Multiple named stores are managed through a thread-safe registry (`get_store()` / `get_default_store()`).

## Core Types

- `ConfigStore` — main class; one instance per JSON file
- `Connection` — RAII handle returned by listener registration; auto-disconnects on destruction
- `StoreOptions` — aggregate options bundle (`path_type`, `save`, `on_missing`, `format`, `env_prefix`)
- `SaveError` — exception thrown when an auto-save disk write fails
- `Path` (enum) — `Relative`, `Absolute`, `AppData`
- `SaveStrategy` (enum) — `Auto` (save on every set), `Manual`
- `MissingKeyPolicy` (enum) — `DefaultValue`, `ThrowException`
- `JsonFormat` (enum) — `Pretty`, `Compact`
- `Encoding` (enum) — `None`, `Base64`, `Hex`, `ROT13`, `Reverse`, `Combined`

## Key APIs

### ConfigStore construction

- `ConfigStore(path, type, save, policy)` — positional constructor
- `ConfigStore(path, StoreOptions)` — named-options constructor

### Reading

- `get<T>(key, default_value)` — read with fallback; returns default if key absent
- `get<T>(key)` — read; behavior on missing key controlled by `MissingKeyPolicy`
- `get_root<T>(default_value)` / `get_root<T>()` — deserialize the entire root
- `get_or_set<T>(key, default_value)` — atomic read-or-initialize
- `get_all<T>(prefix)` — returns `unordered_map<string, T>` of immediate children
- `contains(key)` — returns `bool`; `[[nodiscard]]`
- `sub(prefix)` — point-in-time `nlohmann::json` snapshot of a subtree
- `all_keys(prefix)` — recursive leaf-key enumeration; `[[nodiscard]]`
- `keys(prefix)` — immediate child keys (shallow)
- `children(prefix)` — immediate child object keys
- `dump(JsonFormat)` — serialize the whole store to a JSON string

### Writing

- `set(key, value)` — write a value; auto-saves if strategy is Auto; throws `SaveError` on failure
- `set(key, value, Encoding)` — write with per-key obfuscation (stored encoded, read decoded)
- `set_root<T>(value)` — overwrite the entire root with a serializable object
- `remove(key)` — delete a key; throws `SaveError` on auto-save failure
- `clear()` — delete all keys; throws `SaveError` on auto-save failure

### Persistence & lifecycle

- `save()` / `save(JsonFormat)` — flush to disk
- `reload()` — re-read the file from disk (applies env overrides and defaults after load)
- `merge(json)` — deep-merge a JSON object overlay
- `merge_file(path, Path)` — deep-merge from a file
- `load_layered(paths, Path)` — priority-ordered file stacking (later files win)

### Change listeners

- `connect(key, callback)` — raw `json` listener; returns `Connection`
- `on_change<T>(key, callback)` — typed listener; returns `Connection`
- `on_any_change(callback)` — wildcard raw listener; returns `Connection`
- `on_any_change<T>(callback)` — wildcard typed listener; returns `Connection`

### Background watcher & validation

- `start_watch(interval)` — poll file for changes; calls `reload()` automatically (default 1 s)
- `stop_watch()` — stop background watcher thread
- `set_validator(fn)` — register a `void(const json&)` callback; throw inside it to reject config
- `clear_validator()` — remove the validator

### Defaults & environment

- `set_default<T>(key, value)` — in-memory fallback; survives `reload()` and `clear()`
- `clear_defaults()` — remove all registered defaults
- `bind_env(key, env_var)` — map a specific environment variable to a config key

### Global registry

- `get_store(path, type, save, policy)` — retrieve-or-create a cached `ConfigStore`
- `get_store(path, StoreOptions)` — retrieve-or-create with options bundle
- `get_default_store()` — the implicit store used by global free functions
- `release_store(path)` — remove one store from the cache
- `release_all_stores()` — clear the entire registry

## Usage Patterns

### Global quick-start

```cpp
#include <config/config.hpp>

config::set("server/host", std::string("127.0.0.1"));
config::set("server/port", 8080);
auto host = config::get<std::string>("server/host", "localhost");
auto port = config::get<int>("server/port", 80);
```

No setup required. Writes to `config.json` in the working directory.

### ConfigStore with options

```cpp
config::ConfigStore store("myapp/settings.json", {
    .path_type  = config::Path::AppData,
    .save       = config::SaveStrategy::Manual,
    .env_prefix = "MYAPP_",   // MYAPP_SERVER_PORT -> server/port
});
store.set_default("server/port", 8080);
store.set("server/port", 9090);
store.save();
```

### Layered config (defaults → environment → local override)

```cpp
store.load_layered({
    "config/defaults.json",
    "config/production.json",
    "config/local.json",      // silently skipped if absent
});
```

### RAII change listener

```cpp
// Connection auto-disconnects when it goes out of scope.
auto conn = store.on_change<int>("server/port", [](int port) {
    std::cout << "Port changed to " << port << "\n";
});
store.set("server/port", 9090);  // fires the listener
// conn destroyed here -> listener removed
```

### Obfuscated values

```cpp
store.set("auth/token", std::string("s3cr3t"), config::Encoding::Base64);
// Stored as Base64 on disk; decoded transparently on get.
auto token = store.get<std::string>("auth/token");
```

## Files

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `include/config/config.hpp` | Public entry-point header — include this file |
| `include/config/store.hpp` | Full `ConfigStore` implementation, `Connection`, `StoreOptions`, `SaveError`, global free functions, and the store registry |
| `include/config/detail/types.hpp` | Enum definitions (`Path`, `SaveStrategy`, `MissingKeyPolicy`, `JsonFormat`, `Encoding`) and `CONFIG_STRUCT` / `CONFIG_STRUCT_WITH_DEFAULT` macros |
| `include/config/detail/obfuscation.hpp` | `ObfuscationEngine` — encode/decode helpers for Base64, Hex, ROT13, Reverse, Combined |
| `include/config/detail/path_resolver.hpp` | `resolve_path()` — platform-aware path resolution (Relative / Absolute / AppData) |
| `docs/API_Reference.md` | Complete API reference with all overloads and parameter descriptions |
| `docs/Examples.md` | Annotated usage examples covering all major features |
